page: Vultr vbm-48c-1024gb-4-a100-gpu is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It is equipped with an x86_64 architecture Intel Xeon 6342 processor featuring 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The system is configured with 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 10.67 GB of RAM per core. For storage, the server includes a 900 GB local SSD. It also comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The primary accelerator hardware consists of four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, delivering a combined total of 320 GB of VRAM. This dense GPU configuration is designed for highly parallelized workloads. A notable hardware tradeoff is the local storage capacity; the 900 GB SSD is relatively compact compared to the massive 1024.0 GB of system memory and the 320 GB of GPU VRAM, which may require external storage solutions for larger datasets.

Given the hardware profile, this bare metal server is built for intensive computational tasks. The combination of 96 vCPUs, 1024.0 GB of RAM, and quad NVIDIA A100 GPUs makes it suitable for deep learning, large-scale AI model training, complex data science jobs, and high-performance computing simulations. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare metal deployment eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the return on the high-density GPU and memory resources.
description: Vultr vbm-48c-1024gb-4-a100-gpu is a high-density bare metal server designed for intensive computational workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 6342 processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, a 900 GB local SSD, and one complimentary public IPv4 address. Acceleration is driven by four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 320 GB of VRAM. While the 900 GB local SSD is modest relative to the massive memory and GPU capacity, the bare metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead to maximize resource efficiency. This configuration is engineered for parallel processing tasks, including deep learning, AI model training, data science, and high-performance computing simulations.
